text.title: Agentic Workflow for Blurred Roles
text.summary: How to use role-persona subagents, audit patterns, and key gotchas when AI agents operate in overlapping-role zones.

rule.statement: Assign each Venn-overlap zone (PM/design, eng/discovery, data/product) to one agent role with a single human DRI. Agents run parallel disciplinary lenses; the human DRI converges.
rule.rationale: Parallel agents without convergence produce contradictory PRDs, evals, and GTM briefs with no owner. The converge step is mandatory, not optional.

example.note: Use faion-brainstorm to spin a diverge phase across PM-agent, designer-agent, data-agent, and eng-agent on the same problem. Each agent produces only its disciplinary lens. The human DRI reads all four and makes the call.
Four subagents all given write access to the same PRD produce four contradictory PRDs and no decision. Always attach a converge step with a named human DRI.

rule.statement: For role-evolution audits, run a researcher-style agent that maps current responsibilities to target Venn zones and emits a gap list, then file SDD tasks per gap.
rule.rationale: Manual role mapping is biased toward self-perception. Agent-driven mapping from git, Linear, and CODEOWNERS produces evidence-based gaps rather than aspirational ones.

example.code: Audit current team T against the blurred-roles model. For each member, list (a) primary discipline, (b) secondary fluencies, (c) overlap zones they participate in, (d) gaps. Output a CSV plus 3 prioritized SDD tasks to close the largest gap. Do not propose firing or restructuring.

antipattern.description: Running four role-persona agents without a human DRI convergence step.
antipattern.reason: The loudest or first agent's recommendation ships. Convergence is the mechanism that turns parallel lenses into a decision.

antipattern.description: PM-agent given an engineering codebase with no tool constraints.
antipattern.reason: Agents inherit the prompt's role boundary but not skill limits. Constrain via tool allow-lists per persona, not prose instructions alone.

antipattern.description: Using agent-driven skill assessments ("rate this PM on data fluency from their PRDs") as performance inputs.
antipattern.reason: Assessments are noisy and biased toward verbose writers. Use as discussion seeds only; never as performance evidence.

antipattern.description: Cross-role knowledge transfer between agents without a PII filter.
antipattern.reason: The data-agent may surface customer PII in a design-agent prompt. Run a scrubber filter on inter-agent handoffs that cross role boundaries.

list.item: DRI assignment per overlap zone — documented in the role doc, not tribal knowledge
Approval of any role-restructure SDD task generated by the audit agent
Final sign-off on hiring scorecards produced by agents
